pattern: Sort the product list once; for each growing prefix of the search word, binary-search for the first product that is lexicographically >= the prefix, then collect up to three consecutive matches whose prefix still holds.
hints: Sort products lexicographically once upfront — after sorting, all products that share a given prefix form a contiguous block, so a single binary search locates the start of each block in O(log n).

For prefix p, the first candidate is the leftmost product >= p in sorted order; scan forward from there collecting products that start with p, stopping after three or when the prefix no longer matches.

The prefix grows by one character each step — you can either re-run binary search from scratch each time (O(k log n) total) or advance a left pointer since the block can only move right as the prefix lengthens.

edge_inputs.description: single product that matches every prefix of the search word
no product shares any prefix with the search word — all sublists empty
five products from the canonical example — suggestions narrow with each character
products list has more than three matches for the first prefix — only top three returned
single-character search word — one prefix step only
products longer than search word still match their prefix
search word longer than all products — sublists empty once prefix exceeds product length
all products are identical — three copies suggested for every prefix
products unsorted in input — sorting step is essential

stress.description: 1000 products of length 20 from a small alphabet, search word length 15 — many prefix collisions stress the binary search boundary logic
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    alphabet = 'abcde'
    products = list({''.join(rng.choice(list(alphabet)) for _ in range(20)) for _ in range(n * 2)})[:n]
    search_word = ''.join(rng.choice(list(alphabet)) for _ in range(15))
    return [products, search_word]

complexity.time: O(n log n + k log n)
